ops.title: ccx MCP tools eager-loaded via per-tool alwaysLoad _meta
ops.body:
The 4 common ccx tools (`ccx_code_read`, `ccx_code_grep`, `ccx_code_outline`, `ccx_code_search`) carry `Meta: mcp.Meta{"anthropic/alwaysLoad": true}` in `internal/mcpserver/server.go` `register()`, so they stay loaded under Claude Code's `ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH` deferral — a guard redirect to one costs no ToolSearch round-trip. The rest of the surface stays deferred.

**Verified mechanics (against Claude Code binary 2.1.209 + go-sdk v1.6.1):**
- Deferral predicate short-circuits on alwaysLoad: `if(tool.alwaysLoad===true) return false; ...; if(tool.isMcp===true) return true`. Per-tool source: `alwaysLoad: config.alwaysLoad===true || tool._meta?.["anthropic/alwaysLoad"]===true`. So boolean `true` at `_meta["anthropic/alwaysLoad"]` exempts a tool regardless of ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH.
- **MCP-only.** Native tools (TaskCreate, Monitor, EnterPlanMode, …) take the `isMcp!==true` branch and are deferred by hardcoded name checks — there is NO user-facing alwaysLoad path for them. The capt-hook general-pack ToolSearch preload nudge for native tools is the only mechanism and CANNOT be migrated to alwaysLoad or deleted.
- Chose **per-tool `_meta`** over server-level `.mcp.json alwaysLoad`: server-level loads ALL tools AND makes that server's connect block startup (≤5s). Per-tool is surgical + non-blocking.
- go-sdk plumbing: `mcp.Tool` embeds `Meta` (`type Meta map[string]any`, json `_meta,omitempty`). `mcp.AddTool`→`toolForErr` does `tt := *t` (value copy), preserving Meta into the registered tool; `tools/list` surfaces it and the client round-trips it. `TestAlwaysLoadMetaSurface` asserts alwaysLoad ⟺ the 4-name allowlist over the full ListTools surface.

Keep the set minimal — every alwaysLoad tool costs upfront context on every session, the direct tension with ccx's context-minimization thesis. Related: [[cc-context-architecture]].
